senses.definition: A benzofuranone-type heterocycle used as an intermediate in organic synthesis.
examples: Benzalphthalide is prepared as a key starting material for phthalazinone derivatives.
Researchers used benzalphthalide to access antifungal-related compounds.

senses.definition: A shortened form or plural referring to benzaldehyde derivatives or related benzyl compounds in some chemical contexts; specific meaning varies by subfield.
examples: The synthesis uses several benzals as intermediates.
Benzals are often prepared from benzaldehyde derivatives.

senses.definition: A white crystalline compound, C6H5CONH2, the amide of benzoic acid; used as a chemical reagent and in synthesis.
examples: Benzamide acts as a building block in organic synthesis.
The benzamide intermediate was purified before further reactions.

senses.definition: Having a benzamido group (a benzoyl amide moiety) attached in a chemical structure.
examples: The benzamido substituent was introduced to modify the activity.
Benzamido derivatives showed altered solubility.

senses.definition: A chemical compound, an amine derived from benzaldehyde; used in organic synthesis and as a reagent.
examples: Benzamine was used to form new imine linkages.
The reaction mixture contained benzamine as a key amine source.

senses.definition: Relating to or containing benzamine-like structures or properties.
examples: Benzaminic ligands showed strong coordination behavior.
The benzaminic framework influenced the reaction selectivity.

senses.definition: A naming element indicating a benzamino group; used in systematic chemical nomenclature.
examples: The benzamino substituent was introduced in the aromatic ring.
Benzamino-phenyl compounds were synthesized.

senses.definition: A chemical term likely referring to a benz-an anomeric system or a historical/trade name; not widely used in modern standard dictionaries.
examples: Benzanlagen is found in older synthesis literature.

senses.definition: An organic amide formed from benzanilide; used in pharmaceutical and dye chemistry.
examples: Benzanilide derivatives are studied for biological activity.
The benzanilide scaffold is common in dye chemistry.

senses.definition: A polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon composed of a benzene ring fused to an anthracene structure; used in organic electronics and spectroscopy.
examples: Benzanthracene is studied for its photophysical properties.
Benzenanthracene derivatives are used in OLED materials.

senses.definition: Plural of benzanthracene; multiple related compounds in the same chemical family.
examples: The library contains several benzanthracenes with different substitutions.

senses.definition: A polycyclic aromatic ketone used in dye chemistry and fluorescence studies.
examples: Benzanthrone derivatives are used as fluorescent tags.
The benzanthrone core is common in optical probes.

senses.definition: A specific aldehyde oxime derivative with a benzene ring; used in specialized organic synthesis.
examples: The route involves benzantialdoxime as an intermediate.

senses.definition: A chemical compound containing the benzene ring fused to a diazide or azide group; used in organic synthesis.
examples: Benzazide was employed in cycloaddition reactions.

senses.definition: A benzene-containing imide compound used in specialized synthesis.
examples: Benzazimide serves as a scaffold in novel compounds.

senses.definition: A heterocyclic compound containing two nitrogen atoms in a fused benzazine ring system; used in chemical research.
examples: Benzazine derivatives show interesting binding properties.

senses.definition: A class of nitrogen-containing bicyclic compounds with a benzene ring fused to another heterocycle; used in medicinal chemistry.
examples: Benzazole rings are common in kinase inhibitors.

senses.definition: A benzene ring fused to a triazole-containing moiety; used in advanced materials or synthesis.
examples: Benzbitriazole units can act as light-absorbing components.

senses.definition: A diazine ring fused to a benzene system; used in organic synthesis and medicinal chemistry.
examples: Benzo[diazine] structures are explored as potential therapeutics.

senses.definition: A fused benzene–difuran system; known in specialized organic chemistry contexts.
examples: Benzi- ... difuran derivatives were synthesized.

senses.definition: A benzene fused with dioxazine ring; used in advanced materials and dye chemistry.
examples: Benzo-dioxazine structures contribute to color properties.

senses.definition: A complex fused heterocycle with multiple oxygen and nitrogen atoms; used in niche chemical literature.
examples: The benzdioxdiazine scaffold appears in specialized syntheses.

senses.definition: A triazine-containing fused ring system with a benzene backbone; used in specialty synthesis.
examples: Benzo-diox-triazine derivatives are explored for electronic properties.

senses.definition: A stimulant drug historically derived from amphetamine; used medically in the past and known for abuse potential.
examples: Benzedrine was once prescribed for narcolepsy and fatigue.
Benzedrine abuse led to regulatory control in many countries.

senses.definition: A chemical term found in some compound names; may refer to a benzene-derived reagent or placeholder in older literature.
examples: Benzein is listed as a component in the reaction scheme.
